description: A use-after-free vulnerability exists in V8's JSDispatchEntry lifecycle management when the LeapTiering feature is enabled. The root cause is that `InstructionStream::BodyDescriptor::kRelocModeMask` in `src/objects/objects-body-descriptors-inl.h` does not include `RelocInfo::ModeMask(RelocInfo::JS_DISPATCH_HANDLE)`. As a result, when the garbage collector iterates the body of an InstructionStream (JITted machine code) object, it skips over `JS_DISPATCH_HANDLE` relocation info entries and never calls `VisitJSDispatchTableEntry` for dispatch handles that are referenced exclusively through JITted code's reloc info. This means a JSDispatchEntry referenced only by Maglev-compiled code's relocation info is not marked as alive during GC. An attacker can exploit this by: (1) using an in-sandbox write primitive to zero out all in-sandbox references to a target dispatch entry (JSFunction.dispatch_handle and FeedbackCell.dispatch_handle), (2) triggering GC to free the now-unreferenced dispatch entry, (3) allocating a new function whose dispatch entry reuses the freed slot but with a different parameter count. Stale Maglev-compiled code that previously recorded the dispatch handle in reloc info continues to call through the entry using the old parameter count, causing a stack imbalance that leads to RIP/PC control — a sandbox violation enabling arbitrary code execution.

fixes.evidence_text: [heap] Fix processing of JSDispatch handle through RelocInfo
fixes.description: Local patch exported from https://crrev.com/c/6939390 for reproducible fixed-image builds.
